properties.description: An indescribable tangle of blood-swollen eyes, writhing limbs, and a gaping maw, driven by eternal hunger.
No matter how withered the victim's body may be, the ghast curse is capable of sustaining a shadow of life and consciousness within it for decades on end.
A small-time heretic hoping to elevate his standing in the cult through the diligent study of blood magic.
A small-time heretic hoping to elevate his standing in the cult through the diligent study of blood magic.
The heretical whisperings, resounding hollowly through the shadowy undercrofts, are blasphemous - yet persuasive enough to sow a seed of doubt...
Fully embracing the Gift requires a strength of conviction that few actually possess.
Fully embracing the Gift requires a strength of conviction that few actually possess.
The combination of crossbows and ambushes is almost foolproof - and the arbalesters take full advantage of it.
The combination of crossbows and ambushes is almost foolproof - and the arbalesters take full advantage of it.
Before finally succumbing to long-awaited death, the most insidious of necromancers may be condemned to linger for centuries as ghasts.

These gluttonous monstrosities rarely leave the forests, but when they do, it usually ends in disaster. Their astonishing strength and toughness make them a threat that only experienced, well-armed warriors stand any chance against.
The only thing keeping this vaguely human-shaped product of vile rituals alive is its unwavering faith in the Court.
The prophets marked by the Court itself can no longer be called either men or beasts: their mangled sermons are understood only by monstrosities akin to them.
Apostates are former clerics of the Host, driven insane by the apocalyptic visions of their new patron. They are tasked with choosing which cultists are worthy of receiving the Gift.
Amid the halls heaped with corpses, a newborn faith painfully takes shape, its first commandments set in blood by mad prophets.
This warlock has devoted his life to the search for and study of occult manuscripts - and he'll stop at nothing to acquire the next forbidden text.
A leader of the vampiric cult occupying this monastery. The Stoneshard in his hands doesn't bode well...
This monster is only capable of feeling two things: pain and animalistic fury, fueled by the Stoneshard lodged within.
Large brigand gangs operate much like small armies - disciplined and with their own strict hierarchies, symbols, and flags.
Once native to Aldor's shadowy groves, this ancient bat species now survives only in the depths of caves.
Bears may not be the most aggressive of beasts, but they quickly lose their temper when a trespasser enters their territory.
This avid hunter had an epiphany: watching people fight for their lives is far more entertaining to him than stalking wildlife.
This avid hunter had an epiphany: watching people fight for their lives is far more entertaining to him than stalking wildlife.
In olden days, huge bison herds grazed all over Aldor, but thanks to the efforts of overly determined hunters, few of them remain to roam the land.
A chaotically pulsating manifestation of boiling blood, defiled flesh, and pernicious magic.
Bloated with blood, these heinous creatures are commanded solely by hunger and the urge to tear and destroy.
Once this monster catches its victim's scent, there is no escape. It will not rest until its quarry is brought down in the most grotesque manner.
May be quite dangerous when angered.
The only thing this thug truly believes is that might makes right. Anyone who stands in his way is bound to deeply regret it.
The only thing this thug truly believes is that might makes right. Anyone who stands in his way is bound to deeply regret it.
For branders, the sight of raging flames is almost as thrilling as the anguish of those around them.
Not every sorcerer manages to find a place at the Academy or in a noble court, which often leads them down the path of crime.
Not every sorcerer manages to find a place at the Academy or in a noble court, which often leads them down the path of crime.
Not every sorcerer manages to find a place at the Academy or in a noble court, which often leads them down the path of crime.
Not every sorcerer manages to find a place at the Academy or in a noble court, which often leads them down the path of crime.
Warlocks aren't particularly picky about their means or their ends. For many of them, the life of banditry is a perfect way to earn some easy coin while honing their magical skill.
A burly fellow, dead set on the belief that every problem can be resolved with intimidation and violence.
Buntings have a distinct preference for building their nests near cultivated fields.
It naps, brings a sense of homely coziness, and occasionally catches mice.
The stench of charred flesh and the clatter of chains weave together into a symphony of perpetual agony.
Exalted both in spirit and in flesh, cherubim are the most beloved servants of the Court.
Exalted both in spirit and in flesh, cherubim are the most beloved servants of the Court.
Chickens aren't picky. All they want from life is a warm coop and a handful of grain.
The Court rewards only its most loyal followers - those who receive the Gift must endure a long, painful adjustment to their new, inhuman bodies.
Owning a cow is a sign of rural prosperity. Only the most well-to-do families can afford one.
Crawlers are hated and feared: one wrong move, one careless sound, and even an experienced hunter becomes the prey. To everyone's relief, crawlers rarely stray far from their burrows.

Few suspect the existence of female crawlers: they prefer to lay their clutches deep within caves, far from most threats. Yet even there, broodmothers guard their offspring with such ferocity that a mere chance encounter with one usually proves fatal.

Crawlers make their burrows in the deep woods, where they hunt small wildlife. Other predators do their best to stay away - crawlers' caustic blood, venomous bites, and ability to almost instantly heal their fragile carapaces turn any encounter into a battle of attrition.
A cold-blooded thug, willing to kill for a fistful of coins... or simply for the thrill of it.
A cold-blooded thug, willing to kill for a fistful of coins... or simply for the thrill of it.
Cursed to endlessly roam the crypt's halls, the keeper's sole duty is to preserve the ghostly light of its candles - a twisted mockery of Hieronite burial customs.
The neophyte tasked with guarding you. He hasn't been doing a very good job so far.
...It tolls for thee.
This scoundrel is skilled with his daggers and, most importantly, enjoys putting them to use.
This scoundrel is skilled with his daggers and, most importantly, enjoys putting them to use.
Their lasting torment and suffering may have finally come to an end, but even in death, the martyrs continue to serve the Court.
A hive of deathstingers. Better not disturb it.
Deathstingers are the scourge of the local woods. A large swarm of these fist-sized insects can make short work of anything - or anyone.
Deer are among the most plentiful inhabitants of the local woods.
A once-indomitable will and a keen intellect, confined within this half-dead body, could have served the greater good - were it not for the pull of forbidden knowledge.
Some soldiers are sick of serving in the armies of either the Council or the Magistrate. Banding together and robbing innocents - now that's where the real excitement lies.
Some soldiers are sick of serving in the armies of either the Council or the Magistrate. Banding together and robbing innocents - now that's where the real excitement lies.
Some soldiers are sick of serving in the armies of either the Council or the Magistrate. Banding together and robbing innocents - now that's where the real excitement lies.
Some soldiers are sick of serving in the armies of either the Council or the Magistrate. Banding together and robbing innocents - now that's where the real excitement lies.
Some soldiers are sick of serving in the armies of either the Council or the Magistrate. Banding together and robbing innocents - now that's where the real excitement lies.
Some soldiers are sick of serving in the armies of either the Council or the Magistrate. Banding together and robbing innocents - now that's where the real excitement lies.
Some soldiers are sick of serving in the armies of either the Council or the Magistrate. Banding together and robbing innocents - now that's where the real excitement lies.
At journey's end, some pilgrims discover truths more dreadful than any nightmare they could conjure.
At journey's end, some pilgrims discover truths more dreadful than any nightmare they could conjure.
At journey's end, some pilgrims discover truths more dreadful than any nightmare they could conjure.
Man's most loyal companion.
The rhythm of a war drum can awaken something primal even in hearts usually driven by cowardice.
Compared to other poultry, ducks are much more fickle and demanding, so they are rarely farmed in this part of the world.
For some, dueling has little to do with honor, serving only as a means of cruel, blood-pumping entertainment.
A chief adherent of the Proselytes' tenets, zealously overseeing every dogma and ritual.
This dimwitted brute's remarkable strength thoroughly compensates for his lack of smarts.
This dimwitted brute's remarkable strength thoroughly compensates for his lack of smarts.
This dimwitted brute's remarkable strength thoroughly compensates for his lack of smarts.
Suffering and the close presence of death can sometimes lead to revelations that no other path can provide.
Suffering and the close presence of death can sometimes lead to revelations that no other path can provide.
Dogs suffer from war just as much as people: many were left without owners or driven into the wild by starvation. The ones that survived no longer resemble the pets they once were.
This Proselyte is a perfect soldier: inhumanly strong, ferocious, feeling neither pain nor fear, and showing no mercy.
This Proselyte is a perfect soldier: inhumanly strong, ferocious, feeling neither pain nor fear, and showing no mercy.
Leaving nothing behind but smoldering ruins can be an effective strategy - no witnesses, no loose ends.
Pain is the ultimate proof of devotion: flagellants exist in a state of relentless self-torment, hoping to earn their deity's favor.
A shapeless mass, festering with life: it swells and grows even after being severed from the body.
Wounds close, bones knit, and raw flesh sprouts new tentacles before your very eyes.
Its pelt is valuable indeed.
Consumed by the urge to hunt, avenge, and mete out judgement, she'll be stopped by death alone.
Necromancy is a heavy burden, and not every warlock is capable of bearing it. Those who succumb to madness become ghasts - malicious creatures consumed by an obsession with death.

People cursed during their lifetime turn into ghouls. Now their hunger can only be satisfied by fresh carrion.
This ghoul has fed recently.
Girruds are too far gone in their disturbing metamorphosis, giving the impression that their repugnant tongues have a mind of their own.
A former peasant who turned to a life of crime in a desperate attempt to avoid starvation. Fodder for unsavory and dangerous tasks.
A former peasant who turned to a life of crime in a desperate attempt to avoid starvation. Fodder for unsavory and dangerous tasks.
A former peasant who turned to a life of crime in a desperate attempt to avoid starvation. Fodder for unsavory and dangerous tasks.
A former peasant who turned to a life of crime in a desperate attempt to avoid starvation. Fodder for unsavory and dangerous tasks.
A former peasant who turned to a life of crime in a desperate attempt to avoid starvation. Fodder for unsavory and dangerous tasks.
A former peasant who turned to a life of crime in a desperate attempt to avoid starvation. Fodder for unsavory and dangerous tasks.
A cross between a bear and a huge cat, the gulon is extraordinarily voracious. Those who meet this unusual creature seldom live to share the tale.
Women are common enough among the Proselytes, though many break under the weight of the ordeal, transforming into raving furies.
Harpies are always on the lookout, ready to defend their nests against any interloper.
Always ravenous, these beasts are a constant menace to livestock and solitary travelers alike.

Both the Council and the Magistrate were more than happy to bolster their armies with mercenary crossbowmen. After the truce, many of them were left without work - it didn't take long before they turned to alternative ways of making ends meet.

Both the Council and the Magistrate were more than happy to bolster their armies with mercenary crossbowmen. After the truce, many of them were left without work - it didn't take long before they turned to alternative ways of making ends meet.
His prowess with a sling is enough to put even veteran archers to shame.
His prowess with a sling is enough to put even veteran archers to shame.
His prowess with a sling is enough to put even veteran archers to shame.
His prowess with a sling is enough to put even veteran archers to shame.
Despite their small size, hedgehogs are brave, inquisitive little critters.
The bandits who survive long enough to earn the respect of their more experienced fellows eventually become the gang leader's henchmen.
The bandits who survive long enough to earn the respect of their more experienced fellows eventually become the gang leader's henchmen.
The bandits who survive long enough to earn the respect of their more experienced fellows eventually become the gang leader's henchmen.
The bandits who survive long enough to earn the respect of their more experienced fellows eventually become the gang leader's henchmen.
The bandits who survive long enough to earn the respect of their more experienced fellows eventually become the gang leader's henchmen.
The bandits who survive long enough to earn the respect of their more experienced fellows eventually become the gang leader's henchmen.
This wandering sorcerer isn't encumbered by a strict moral code and is willing to offer his services to anyone, as long as the payment is right.
This wandering sorcerer isn't encumbered by a strict moral code and is willing to offer his services to anyone, as long as the payment is right.
Pulling plows, carrying goods, serving as mounts - the entire kingdom depends on the hardy local horse breeds.
Through a special ritual, its soul was stripped of memories and self-awareness, then returned to the body to imbue it with unnatural strength and durability.
Unspeakable torture has left this crazed cultist with a single desire - to die for a higher purpose.

Impalers are unmatched in their ability to track down and capture those poor souls who unknowingly wander into the catacombs. The victims who survive the hunt either join the cult or serve as sacrifices to the Court.
The skittishness of jays is perhaps the main reason for their large population.
Fortunately, Proselytes almost never leave their catacombs - otherwise, even city walls wouldn't be enough to stop the onslaught of juggernauts.

People cursed during their lifetime turn into ghouls. Now their hunger can only be satisfied by fresh carrion.
This ghoul has grown rather fat.
Bloated with blood, these heinous creatures are commanded solely by hunger and the urge to tear and destroy.
An aberration this vile could spring only from pure hatred - hatred for the natural order and life itself.
Proselyte rituals diligently mirror Hieronite rites, albeit in a thoroughly twisted form.
It's said that serving a sentence in the Aldorian quarries can break even the strongest minds, to say nothing of sadists already drawn to pain and blood.
Sorcerers can be troublesome opponents, but this bounty hunter knows their every trick inside and out.
Sorcerers can be troublesome opponents, but this bounty hunter knows their every trick inside and out.

When the war first began, the Magistrate's promises of cutting the nobles down to size and granting everyone a plethora of freedoms seemed appealing to many. The realization that the rich had simply replaced the old nobility only came after the truce.

When the war first began, the Magistrate's promises of cutting the nobles down to size and granting everyone a plethora of freedoms seemed appealing to many. The realization that the rich had simply replaced the old nobility only came after the truce.

When the war first began, the Magistrate's promises of cutting the nobles down to size and granting everyone a plethora of freedoms seemed appealing to many. The realization that the rich had simply replaced the old nobility only came after the truce.
Hunting wild ducks with hawks and dogs has long been a cherished pastime of the nobility.
A nexus of pure Aether energy, delicate and volatile.
A skilled and cruel headhunter who doesn't shy away from using the most dastardly tactics to catch his prey.
A spawn of ancient magic that combines the ferocity of a wild beast with the craftiness of a skilled warlock.
A commoner or deserter who somehow got hold of decent gear and then went on a spree across the realm.
A commoner or deserter who somehow got hold of decent gear and then went on a spree across the realm.
A commoner or deserter who somehow got hold of decent gear and then went on a spree across the realm.
A commoner or deserter who somehow got hold of decent gear and then went on a spree across the realm.
A commoner or deserter who somehow got hold of decent gear and then went on a spree across the realm.
A commoner or deserter who somehow got hold of decent gear and then went on a spree across the realm.
This brigand's aim is so precise he could shoot an apple off his own son's head without blinking an eye.
This brigand's aim is so precise he could shoot an apple off his own son's head without blinking an eye.
The sight of martyrs' agony only strengthens other Proselytes' loyalty to the Court. How deep into madness does one have to descend to volunteer for something like this?
The fervor of Proselyte women often earns them reverence bordering on worship. The most zealous among them rise to become matriarchs, first in line to receive the Gift.
Swinging a pickaxe is hard, thankless labor - nothing this fellow of humble origins isn't accustomed to.
The moose is the sovereign of the Aldorian forests. Encountering one of these mighty beasts is a rare occurrence.
The withered fingers of the mortician have brushed against the bones of peasants, priests, knights, and kings. To those who serve death, it's all the same.
The horrific murkstalkers resemble underground bats not only in appearance but also in behavior, taking full advantage of the catacombs' impenetrable shadows.
Biding their time in the depths of profaned temples, nakkatars are the future monarchs of the coming kingdom of utter darkness.
One of the hierarchs of the secret cult growing in strength in Aldor, he has mastered the art of raising the dead to perfection.
Few believe as blindly - or as fanatically - as the newly converted.
Few believe as blindly - or as fanatically - as the newly converted.
Few believe as blindly - or as fanatically - as the newly converted.
Studying the occult is a slippery slope with few chances to turn back. Sooner or later, anyone who fails to restrain their curiosity becomes a slave to it instead.
The cultists whose bodies rejected the Gift become outcasts. Driven to madness, they drag out a miserable existence as little more than fighting animals.
Highwaymen firmly follow one simple rule: shoot first, ask questions later.
Highwaymen firmly follow one simple rule: shoot first, ask questions later.
His guiding principle in life is to always have someone else handle the dirty work.
A lone hunter and the monarch of the nightly canopy.
When dealing with mercenaries, there's one simple rule to keep in mind: if you can't solve a problem with money, solve it with a lot of money.
A mysterious guest from beyond the Veil - either a reflection of the caster or an entity mimicking their form.
The war wasn't kind to Aldorian pigs: those that didn't starve to death were largely appropriated by marauding soldiers.
Pigeons are often used in large cities and castles to deliver messages. A well-trained carrier pigeon is worth its weight in silver.
Poachers were at odds with the law even before the war, secretly hunting on the lands of the nobility.
Poachers were at odds with the law even before the war, secretly hunting on the lands of the nobility.
The war drags on, and a great number of mines remain deserted. When not busy with raids, many brigand gangs don't hesitate to scour them for any remaining precious ores.
The decay is so far gone that even magic can no longer halt it. A couple more weeks, and the maggot-infested flesh will begin to peel away, exposing bare bones beneath.
The decay is so far gone that even magic can no longer halt it. A couple more weeks, and the maggot-infested flesh will begin to peel away, exposing bare bones beneath.
The decay is so far gone that even magic can no longer halt it. A couple more weeks, and the maggot-infested flesh will begin to peel away, exposing bare bones beneath.
The decay is so far gone that even magic can no longer halt it. A couple more weeks, and the maggot-infested flesh will begin to peel away, exposing bare bones beneath.
The decay is so far gone that even magic can no longer halt it. A couple more weeks, and the maggot-infested flesh will begin to peel away, exposing bare bones beneath.
The decay is so far gone that even magic can no longer halt it. A couple more weeks, and the maggot-infested flesh will begin to peel away, exposing bare bones beneath.
Fluffy and harmless.
This crafty blackguard's arsenal is stocked with grand promises, sweet half-truths, and bold-faced lies.
Above all, this villain's gang relies on the element of surprise - that's why their hideout is set well out of sight.
A man's misery is a rat's good fortune: never before has fresh carrion been so plentiful.
Most people dislike ravens, viewing them as an ill omen of death, war, and grief.
Even the meekest of folks may take up arms when faced with poverty and the prospect of hungry death.
The Gift is only the first of many trials awaiting the most faithful of Proselytes. Suffice to say, not everyone manages to pass them...
In times of war, chivalry and loyalty are but empty words. Personal gain and staying well-fed take much higher priority.
In times of war, chivalry and loyalty are but empty words. Personal gain and staying well-fed take much higher priority.
In times of war, chivalry and loyalty are but empty words. Personal gain and staying well-fed take much higher priority.
In times of war, chivalry and loyalty are but empty words. Personal gain and staying well-fed take much higher priority.
In times of war, chivalry and loyalty are but empty words. Personal gain and staying well-fed take much higher priority.
The restless are usually raised by novice necromancers, as it's much easier to rekindle life in those who died fairly recently.
Attempting to plunder an ancient crypt seldom goes according to plan...
Attempting to plunder an ancient crypt seldom goes according to plan...
This restless is doomed to hunt those he once swore to protect.
This restless is doomed to hunt those he once swore to protect.
This restless is doomed to hunt those he once swore to protect.
This restless is doomed to hunt those he once swore to protect.
This restless is doomed to hunt those he once swore to protect.
This restless is doomed to hunt those he once swore to protect.
Honor, valor, suffering, and rage - all melded together in the fading consciousness of the restless hero, bracing for the final stand in his own tomb.
Honor, valor, suffering, and rage - all melded together in the fading consciousness of the restless hero, bracing for the final stand in his own tomb.
At times, village folk attempt to fight the undead on their own, only to swell their ranks instead.
At times, village folk attempt to fight the undead on their own, only to swell their ranks instead.
At times, village folk attempt to fight the undead on their own, only to swell their ranks instead.
At times, village folk attempt to fight the undead on their own, only to swell their ranks instead.
At times, village folk attempt to fight the undead on their own, only to swell their ranks instead.

He may be dead, mindless, and robbed of free will, but his hands still remember how to wield a weapon.
He may be dead, mindless, and robbed of free will, but his hands still remember how to wield a weapon.
He may be dead, mindless, and robbed of free will, but his hands still remember how to wield a weapon.

He may be dead, mindless, and robbed of free will, but his hands still remember how to wield a weapon.
He may be dead, mindless, and robbed of free will, but his hands still remember how to wield a weapon.
One of the gang's most cunning and ruthless brigands, egging the others on to commit fresh villainies.
One of the gang's most cunning and ruthless brigands, egging the others on to commit fresh villainies.
One of the gang's most cunning and ruthless brigands, egging the others on to commit fresh villainies.
One of the gang's most cunning and ruthless brigands, egging the others on to commit fresh villainies.
Their fragmented minds make it difficult for the undead to use ranged weapons. Nevertheless, they will keep on shooting until the bitter end.
Their fragmented minds make it difficult for the undead to use ranged weapons. Nevertheless, they will keep on shooting until the bitter end.
Dead for thousands of years, now brought back to serve Axonia one last time.
What this skeleton lacks in accuracy, it more than makes up for with an utter absence of fear and hesitation.
What this skeleton lacks in accuracy, it more than makes up for with an utter absence of fear and hesitation.
Only a century ago, the high clergy wielded power that even kings had to reckon with. What would the Order's founders think of the Hieron today?..
The people of Aldor bury their highborn warriors in full battle gear so that they can defend themselves in the afterlife. And defend they do...
The people of Aldor bury their highborn warriors in full battle gear so that they can defend themselves in the afterlife. And defend they do...
The people of Aldor bury their highborn warriors in full battle gear so that they can defend themselves in the afterlife. And defend they do...
Though the crossbow in this skeleton's hands threatens to crumble to dust at any moment, each bolt launched from it remains as deadly as ever.
Though the crossbow in this skeleton's hands threatens to crumble to dust at any moment, each bolt launched from it remains as deadly as ever.
A paragon of valor in life, a slave to a heretic mage in death... It's difficult to imagine a crueler fate for this former knight.
A paragon of valor in life, a slave to a heretic mage in death... It's difficult to imagine a crueler fate for this former knight.
A paragon of valor in life, a slave to a heretic mage in death... It's difficult to imagine a crueler fate for this former knight.
A paragon of valor in life, a slave to a heretic mage in death... It's difficult to imagine a crueler fate for this former knight.
Once, he faithfully served the royal court. Now, he serves again... under other, less worthy masters.
Once, he faithfully served the royal court. Now, he serves again... under other, less worthy masters.
Once, he faithfully served the royal court. Now, he serves again... under other, less worthy masters.
After death, commanders of the Order were to be embalmed and buried in hidden tombs to protect their bodies from potential defilers. Or was it to better preserve them for some future purpose?..
The Order built countless crypts to grant its followers eternal peace in the afterlife. Sadly, this practice became a great boon to the necromantic cults that emerged centuries later.
A stark reminder that righteousness in life and unwavering devotion to one's vows do not guarantee peace in death.
Raised to arms once more by a necromancer's command, this skeleton is perfectly capable of dispatching a timid, inexperienced foe.
Raised to arms once more by a necromancer's command, this skeleton is perfectly capable of dispatching a timid, inexperienced foe.
Raised to arms once more by a necromancer's command, this skeleton is perfectly capable of dispatching a timid, inexperienced foe.
Somewhere deep within his tormented soul, this soldier might even be content to be back at his post.
The people of Aldor bury their highborn warriors in full battle gear so that they can defend themselves in the afterlife. And defend they do...
It was the common soldiers of the Order who bore the brunt of the holy wars, trading their lives for the honor of ending up in a crypt rather than a nameless grave.
It was the common soldiers of the Order who bore the brunt of the holy wars, trading their lives for the honor of ending up in a crypt rather than a nameless grave.
It was the common soldiers of the Order who bore the brunt of the holy wars, trading their lives for the honor of ending up in a crypt rather than a nameless grave.
Raised to arms once more by a necromancer's command, this skeleton is perfectly capable of dispatching a timid, inexperienced foe.
The few specks of life, infused into this fetid, swollen body, are sustained solely by forbidden magic.
Rituals of high necromancy require the caster to give up the most important thing - their free will. Those unwilling to take this necessary step meet their death far sooner than they'd prefer.
Rather than living off their subjects' taxes, certain noblemen find it more profitable and exciting to put together a gang and begin razing neighboring lands.
Rather than living off their subjects' taxes, certain noblemen find it more profitable and exciting to put together a gang and begin razing neighboring lands.
Rather than living off their subjects' taxes, certain noblemen find it more profitable and exciting to put together a gang and begin razing neighboring lands.
Rockeaters typically hunt in pairs - while the soldiers distract the prey, the hunters swiftly sap its life force with poison.
As long as the queen lives, so does the rockeater colony. Given the complexity and length of her life cycle, her death would deal a significant blow to the prosperity of the entire Hive.
With their impenetrable shells and brutal combat instincts, the rockeater soldiers make any Hive incursion an extremely unpleasant surprise - regardless of the cave's original occupants.

The workers have two distinct roles: tirelessly digging tunnels from the main nest in search of unconquered caves, and processing the food brought back by the hunters, making it fit for consumption by the rest of the Hive.
Only two things can turn a knight into a mercenary or a brigand: boredom and poverty.
All too often, faith is nothing more than a noble facade for other, far baser impulses...
In Aldor, the start of a new day is heralded not by a sunrise, but by a rooster's crow.
Sagguls still bear the mark of the Crimson Plague, though for them, it's a blessing rather than a curse.
Herds of saigas are a common sight on the Aldorian steppes, but their remarkable wariness makes them a challenge to hunt.
A heresiarch whose sole purpose is to guide the rest of the cult.
The first to be sent ahead are often the most dispensable. On the upside, those who survive have a good chance to earn the respect of the rest of the gang.
Seagulls are abundant along the Southern Sea's coast, paying no heed to the clamor of big cities.
He has seen much - perhaps too much - and paid the price with his sanity.
Sheep farming is an ancient trade around here: these animals are easy to care for, can graze even on barren lands, and their wool and milk always find a buyer.
Slingers are easy to overlook - right up until the first bruising shot reminds their foes otherwise.
Slingers are easy to overlook - right up until the first bruising shot reminds their foes otherwise.
Slingers are easy to overlook - right up until the first bruising shot reminds their foes otherwise.
Slingers are easy to overlook - right up until the first bruising shot reminds their foes otherwise.
People cursed during their lifetime turn into ghouls. Now their hunger can only be satisfied by fresh carrion.
The common folk have little appreciation for sparrows, thinking of them as annoying, thieving pests.
In folk tales, the heralds are spoken of in hushed tones - their appearance inevitably marks the beginning of a great disaster.
Underground passages are dark and full of terrors: exploring them takes no small measure of grit.

A savvy naturalist might insist that the proper term for spiderlings is instars or nymphs. But since studying crawlers in the wild isn't exactly savvy, it seems unlikely one's around to argue the point.
Bloated with blood, these heinous creatures are commanded solely by hunger and the urge to tear and destroy.
A nimble, thrifty rodent. There must be plenty of nuts stashed in the nearby tree hollows.
He will not hesitate to cast the first stone.
He will not hesitate to cast the first stone.
It can take literally any amount of abuse.
It can take literally any amount of abuse.
It can take literally any amount of abuse.
Soon enough, his pleas will be answered.
Swallows have many superstitions attached to them: people predict the weather by their flight, and a swallow's nest built in the eaves of a house is said to bring good fortune to its residents.
Woe to any wandering knight or Vehement who falls alive into the clutches of the catacomb cults, for the conversion rites forced upon them are cruel beyond measure.
Woe to any wandering knight or Vehement who falls alive into the clutches of the catacomb cults, for the conversion rites forced upon them are cruel beyond measure.
Anmarraks have no trouble growing new tentacles in place of damaged or destroyed ones.
A walking, bloated mound of festering meat, housing an appropriately dark and depraved spirit.
Thrushes have a beautiful singing voice - alas, they are more prized for their tasty, nourishing meat.
A desperate commoner, ready to commit any crime for a chunk of bread and a few coins.

The tit is seen as a symbol of luck, so no harm comes to these birds without good reason - they are hunted only by those stricken with poverty or starvation.
Echoing through the pitch darkness of underground tunnels, the otherworldly tolling of his bell can send even the most reckless hotheads fleeing in terror.
Tormentors praise the Court through devious, sadistic cruelty, reveling in the sight of bloodied flesh.
Tormentors praise the Court through devious, sadistic cruelty, reveling in the sight of bloodied flesh.
While other bandit leaders command through charisma, fear, and a sense of camaraderie, this one believes that fear alone is perfectly sufficient.
Over time, some guards come to the conclusion that robbery can be much more profitable than protecting others from being robbed.

Just as the Gift emerges from suffering, so this creature draws its strength from the fire that devours it from within.
Undertakers wander the world collecting suitable corpses for their experiments and defiling one cemetery after another.
The bite of a viper is deadly, though it strikes only when disturbed.
A hunting dog trained to tear people apart. The constant hunger and abuse turned it into a ferocious monster.
The souls trapped inside will never find peace.
In recent years, wolves have grown terribly ferocious, gathering in great packs and showing no fear of attacking people.
The hound's bloodshot eyes burn with murderous intent and a determination to protect its master until its dying breath.
Wormbearers are the grotesque embodiment of the Court cults: the host suffers from its parasites, while the parasites suffer for their host.
Guests from the beyond are notorious for their inhuman malice and vengefulness.
To be resurrected after death is the most terrible fate that can befall a devout Hieronite, as it forever denies them reunion with the Host.
Leaders of the Order took many of its secrets to their graves, making a commander's sarcophagus a rare and coveted find for any necromancer.
The majority of the Order's initiates were recruited from monasteries, as its commanders found swordsmanship far easier to teach than piety.
Most attempts to comprehend the forbidden come at a price - more often than not, it's paid after death.

The minds of these Order sergeants are clouded by dark magic. Instead of the crumbling hallways of the crypt, they see the narrow streets of Eastern cities - the First Radiant War is in full swing, and by the Host, they won't disgrace their faith.
To earn knighthood, squires of the Order had to prove their courage on the battlefield. Many perished - but now, years after their death, some have been given a second chance.
The inexplicable yearning some templars had for forbidden and occult knowledge went far beyond a mere desire to better understand their enemies.
Legends say that the templars who broke their Oath can never find peace, doomed to return to the living world time and time again.
Summoning a dead man's spirit in its purest form, bypassing the search for a suitable vessel altogether, is the pinnacle of necromantic art.
The superstitions surrounding this small bird are centuries-old: even the ancient Firns held a belief that mischievous forest spirits often adopted the guise of wrens.
This bestial cultist's mind is clouded with unbridled rage - there is very little human left in it.
Trolls never stop growing, and even in their early years, they can already boast formidable size and incredible strength. Luckily for everyone, they multiply at a very slow rate.
There's never been a shortage of souls eager to sacrifice their brethren in the name of a higher cause.
